title: Rewrite a raise-on-429 worker with full return-value protocol
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/rate_limited_worker.ex` currently raises on HTTP 429 responses from Stripe. Rewrite it so: (1) 429 returns `{:snooze, retry_after}`, (2) 404 returns `{:cancel, :customer_not_found}` (the customer was removed upstream, no point retrying), (3) other errors return `{:error, reason}` so Oban's backoff takes over, (4) happy path returns `:ok`. Preserve the `Logger.info` on snooze. Do not raise. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/stripe_sync_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Needs recognition of three distinct error classes (snooze, cancel, retry) plus the deprecation-aware choice of `:cancel` over `:discard`. Sonnet typically gets snooze right but misses the cancel-on-404 nuance.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'When a worker receives a 429 (rate limited) status code, it should return {:snooze, calculate_backoff(attempt)}.'
